skill.name: commit
skill.description: Create commit messages following Sentry conventions. Use when committing code changes, writing commit messages, or formatting git history. Follows conventional commits with Sentry-specific issue references.
skill.summary: Generate compliant commit messages following Sentry engineering practices with conventional commits format
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is pure documentation providing commit message guidelines. Static analysis flagged external_commands, network URLs, and cryptographic patterns, but evaluation confirms these are false positives. The file contains only documentation examples of git commands and reference URLs - no executable code or actual network requests.

content.value_statement: Generate professional commit messages that follow Sentry engineering standards and integrate with their issue tracking system.
actual_capabilities: Generate commit messages following Sentry conventional commits format
Include proper issue references (Sentry, GitHub, Linear)
Apply correct commit types (feat, fix, ref, perf, docs, etc.)
Format commit subject lines within character limits
Add Co-Authored-By attribution for AI-generated changes
limitations: Does not execute git commands - only generates message text
Requires manual review of generated messages before committing
Does not access git history to analyze changes automatically
No integration with git hooks or CI/CD pipelines

use_cases.title: Sentry Developer Workflow
use_cases.description: Create properly formatted commits that automatically link to Sentry issues when merged

use_cases.title: AI Code Review Attribution
use_cases.description: Add proper AI contributor attribution following best practices
use_cases.target_user: Developers using Claude Code or similar AI coding assistants

use_cases.title: Team Convention Enforcement
use_cases.description: Maintain consistent commit history across team repositories
use_cases.target_user: Engineering teams with standardized commit conventions

prompt_templates.title: Simple Feature Commit
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a commit message for adding a new user authentication feature. Reference the Sentry issue SENTRY-1234.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you have a straightforward feature to commit

prompt_templates.title: Bug Fix Commit
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a commit message for fixing a null pointer error in the API endpoint. Include issue reference GH-5678.
prompt_templates.scenario: When committing a bug fix with existing issue tracking

prompt_templates.title: AI-Generated Code Commit
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a commit message for refactoring validation logic. Mark it as AI-generated with Claude attribution.
prompt_templates.scenario: When committing code primarily written by an AI assistant

prompt_templates.title: Breaking Change Commit
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a commit message for removing deprecated API endpoints. Document the breaking change properly.
prompt_templates.scenario: When making changes that break backward compatibility

output_examples.input: Add new Slack integration for alert notifications
output_examples.output:
feat(alerts): Add Slack thread replies for alert updates

When an alert is updated or resolved, post a reply to the original Slack thread instead of creating a new message. This keeps related notifications grouped together.

Refs GH-1234

output_examples.input: Fix null error in user API
output_examples.output:
fix(api): Handle null response in user endpoint

The user API could return null for deleted accounts, causing a crash in the dashboard. Add null check before accessing user properties.

Fixes SENTRY-5678
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

best_practices: Always include an issue reference in the footer to link commits to tracking systems
Use imperative mood in subject lines (Add, Fix, Remove not Added, Fixed, Removed)
Keep subject lines under 70 characters for optimal display in git tools
anti_patterns: Do not use past tense in commit subjects (should be 'Add feature' not 'Added feature')
Avoid generic messages like 'fix bug' or 'update code' without specifics
Do not include AI disclosure phrases in subject or body - only use Co-Authored-By footer

faq.question: What commit types are supported?
faq.answer: Supported types include feat, fix, ref, perf, docs, test, build, ci, chore, style, meta, and license.

faq.question: How do I reference different issue trackers?
faq.answer: Use Fixes or Refs followed by the issue ID: Fixes SENTRY-1234, Fixes GH-1234, or Refs LINEAR-ABC-123.

faq.question: Should I add AI attribution to every commit?
faq.answer: Only add Co-Authored-By when AI tools made significant contributions. Do not add it for minor AI-assisted edits.

faq.question: What is the character limit for commit messages?
faq.answer: Subject line: 70 characters max. Body lines: 100 characters max.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without a Sentry account?
faq.answer: Yes, the commit format works for any project. Sentry issue references are optional.

faq.question: Does this skill run git commands?
faq.answer: No, this skill only generates commit message text. You must manually execute git commit.
